title: Best-of-Majority: Minimax-Optimal Strategy for Pass@k Inference Scaling

methodology.name: Best-of-Majority (BoM)
methodology.description: A minimax-optimal strategy for Pass@k inference scaling
key_features: Generates N responses and returns up to k of them
Establishes a matching lower bound, certifying minimax optimality
Performance does not degrade when increasing N
Unlike majority voting and BoN, BoM maintains performance with increased N
methodology.evaluation: Tested on math problems, showing outperformance of majority voting and BoN

results.experimental_findings: BoM outperforms both majority voting and BoN on math problems
results.theoretical_guarantees: BoM is minimax optimal with matching lower bounds

key_concepts: Pass@k metric: A metric for tasks where any one of k candidate outputs being correct is sufficient
BoN (Best-of-N): An existing method that BoM outperforms
Majority voting: Another existing method that BoM outperforms

introduction.context: Scaling laws in deep neural networks and their application across various domains
introduction.focus: Inference scaling as a complementary paradigm to training-time scaling
introduction.framework: Pass@k inference framework where the algorithm generates N responses and returns up to k of them
